1	"""Generic folder sync job via rsync-over-SSH.
2	
3	Syncs configured folders from source to target, running rsync as root on both
4	ends (local `sudo -E rsync` on source, `--rsync-path='sudo rsync'` on target)
5	to preserve cross-owner file metadata including POSIX ACLs and xattrs.
6	
7	Safety relies on btrfs pre/post snapshots, the rsync --dry-run deletion log,
8	and the orchestrator topology check (ADR-015).  This module is a pure rsync
9	mirror: validate() checks system prerequisites only; execute() runs the
10	rsync transfer.
11	"""
12	
13	from __future__ import annotations
14	
15	import getpass
16	import os
17	import re
18	import shlex
19	from collections.abc import AsyncIterator
20	from dataclasses import dataclass
21	from pathlib import Path, PurePosixPath
22	from typing import Any, ClassVar, override
23	
24	from pcswitcher.disk import format_bytes
25	from pcswitcher.jobs.base import SyncJob
26	from pcswitcher.jobs.vscode_state_sync import vscode_state_exclude_paths
27	from pcswitcher.models import ConfigError, FirstSyncScope, Host, LogLevel, ProgressUpdate, ValidationError
28	
29	# Matches rsync --info=progress2 output, e.g.:
30	#   "9.53G 21% 317.26MB/s 0:00:28 (xfr#83063, to-chk=443926/538653)"
31	#   "29,958,458  99%   27.90GB/s    0:00:00 (xfr#298201, to-chk=1200/300501)"
32	#   "20.000   0%    0,00kB/s    0:00:00 (xfr#1, ir-chk=1039/1101)"
33	# Group 1: size token (e.g. "9.53G" or "29,958,458") — used to compute
34	# bytes_transferred (WR-01).  rsync thousands-groups the plain byte counter with
35	# a locale-dependent separator (',' on C/en_US, '.' on nl_BE-style), so the size
36	# class must accept both: dropping ',' made re.search() stop at the first comma
37	# and capture only the last 1-3 digits (e.g. "29,958,458" -> "458"), skewing
38	# bytes_transferred by orders of magnitude while files-transferred (ungrouped)
39	# stayed correct.
40	# Group 2: rsync's own percentage — captured but NEVER shown.  It is bytes-sent over
41	# the total size of the whole tree, not of the files needing transfer, so an
42	# incremental sync reads 0% from first line to last (measured: 200 of 154,022 files
43	# re-sent, 14.4 MB against a 14.6 GB tree, 0% on all 206 lines).  The bar is driven by
44	# the checked-file counter instead, which spans 0-100% in every case.
45	# Group 3: files transferred so far.
46	# Group 4: chk prefix (`ir` or `to`).  Group 5: files still to check.  Group 6: total.
47	# The trailing counter is `ir-chk=` (incremental-recursion, file list still being
48	# built) or `to-chk=` (full list known).  Both must match: matching only to-chk left
49	# the bar hidden for most of a big first sync (#191), since no match means no
50	# `_report_progress` call and the Rich task is never lazily created.
51	#
52	# The prefix is captured because the two phases differ in signal quality: under ir-chk
53	# group 6 is the entry count discovered *so far*, not a total, so neither it nor any
54	# percentage derived from it may reach the bar.  `--no-inc-recursive` (see
55	# `_build_rsync_cmd`) means ir-chk should never appear at all; `_stream_rsync` keeps a
56	# degraded scanned-count path for it regardless.
57	_PROGRESS2_RE = re.compile(r"(\d+[\d,.]*[KMGT]?)\s+(\d+)%\s+\S+\s+\S+\s+\(xfr#(\d+),\s*(ir|to)-chk=(\d+)/(\d+)\)")
58	
59	# The two rsync passes a folder can run (see `execute`): the no-delete pass that ships
60	# per-directory filter files, and the deleting mirror.  Both share that folder's
61	# progress bar (tracked by path) and only rename it, so the run shows one bar per
62	# configured folder — finished folders stay at 100%.
63	PASS_PREP = "prep"
64	PASS_FULL = "full"
65	
66	
67	def _pass_display(path: str, label: str) -> str:
68	    """Name a pass for the TUI.
69	
70	    The deleting mirror is the pass that always runs, so it shows the bare folder path;
71	    only the optional seeding pass is qualified, so its bar cannot be mistaken for the
72	    real sync of that folder.
73	    """
74	    return path if label == PASS_FULL else f"{path} ({label})"
75	
76	
77	# pc-switcher's own runtime STATE lives under the invoking user's home and must
78	# stay machine-local: sync-history.json is the topology-safety state (ADR-015) that
79	# a --delete mirror would clobber mid-sync; the lock file and logs are per-machine.
80	# See ADR-017 (supersedes ADR-016).
81	#
82	# The install itself (uv tool venv + ~/.local/bin shim) is deliberately NOT
83	# excluded: it mirrors from source like any other uv tool, so the venv and the
84	# interpreter it references travel together and stay consistent. Excluding the venv
85	# while --delete-mirroring the uv interpreter tree it depends on deleted the in-use
86	# interpreter and broke pc-switcher on the target.
87	#
88	# Two GLOBAL-FIRST, non-overridable exclude groups exist, both owned elsewhere or here
89	# and both emitted before the user filter surfaces so no `+` rule can re-expose them:
90	#   1. this runtime-state relpath (below), home-relative and folder_sync's own concern;
91	#   2. the VS Code state DBs (VS Code and its forks), whose ABSOLUTE paths are owned and
92	#      computed by vscode_state_sync (which merges them selectively so machine-bound
93	#      secret:// rows are never clobbered, ADR-018). folder_sync knows nothing about VS Code
94	#      or home layout — it just translates each absolute path from vscode_state_exclude_paths()
95	#      into an rsync filter for the folder being synced (see _vscode_state_exclude_filters).
96	# Every OTHER exclusion is user-configurable.
97	_RUNTIME_EXCLUDE_RELPATHS: tuple[str, ...] = (
98	    ".local/share/pc-switcher",  # lock, sync-history.json, logs/
99	)
100

102	@dataclass
103	class FolderEntry:
104	    """A single folder to sync, with its optional central rsync filter file.
105	
106	    `filter_file` is a per-folder central rsync merge filter file (native
107	    rsync `+`/`-` filter syntax, first-match-wins); unset/empty means no
108	    central merge rule for that folder (runtime excludes and the tree-wide
109	    `dir-merge /.pcswitcher-filter` still apply).
110	    """
111	
112	    path: str
113	    enabled: bool = True
114	    filter_file: str | None = None
115	
116	    def expanded_filter_file(self) -> str | None:
117	        """Return the ~- and env-var-expanded absolute filter_file path, or None if unset.
118	
119	        Expansion uses the invoking user's environment because the source is
120	        always the local machine and rsync reads the merge file locally there
121	        (the merge/dir-merge rules only take effect on the source side of the
122	        transfer; see `_build_rsync_cmd`).
123	        """
124	        if not self.filter_file:
125	            return None
126	        # Path.expanduser() has no env-var equivalent, so expandvars runs first (os.path,
127	        # no pathlib alternative exists), then Path.expanduser() resolves the leading ~.
128	        return str(Path(os.path.expandvars(self.filter_file)).expanduser())
129

131	class FolderSyncJob(SyncJob):
132	    """Generic folder sync via rsync-over-SSH.
133	
134	    Syncs configured folders from source to target, running rsync as root on
135	    both ends to preserve cross-owner file metadata.
136	
137	    Config shape (mirrors config-schema.yaml `folder_sync` section):
138	        folders:
139	          - path: /home
140	            enabled: true          # default
141	            filter_file: ~/...     # central rsync merge filter file, optional
142	    """
143	
144	    name: ClassVar[str] = "folder_sync"
145	
146	    CONFIG_SCHEMA: ClassVar[dict[str, Any]] = {
147	        "type": "object",
148	        "properties": {
149	            "folders": {
150	                "type": "array",
151	                "items": {
152	                    "type": "object",
153	                    "properties": {
154	                        "path": {"type": "string"},
155	                        "enabled": {"type": "boolean", "default": True},
156	                        "filter_file": {
157	                            "type": "string",
158	                            "description": (
159	                                "Path to a central rsync merge filter file for this folder "
160	                                "(~ and env vars expanded); unset means no central filter rule"
161	                            ),
162	                        },
163	                    },
164	                    "required": ["path"],
165	                    "additionalProperties": False,
166	                },
167	                "minItems": 1,
168	            }
169	        },
170	        "required": ["folders"],
171	        "additionalProperties": False,
172	    }
173	
174	    @classmethod
175	    @override
176	    def validate_config(cls, config: dict[str, Any]) -> list[ConfigError]:
177	        """Validate config schema, then that every folder path is absolute.
178	
179	        `path` is handed to rsync verbatim — no `~`/env expansion, unlike
180	        `filter_file` — so a relative path silently resolves against the working
181	        directory of each side and mirrors the wrong tree.
182	        """
183	        errors = super().validate_config(config)
184	        if errors:
185	            return errors  # Don't continue if schema is invalid
186	
187	        for index, folder in enumerate(config["folders"]):
188	            if not PurePosixPath(folder["path"]).is_absolute():
189	                errors.append(
190	                    ConfigError(
191	                        job=cls.name,
192	                        path=f"folders.{index}.path",
193	                        message=f"Folder path must be absolute: {folder['path']!r}",
194	                    )
195	                )
196	
197	        return errors
198	
199	    @staticmethod
200	    def _parse_size_to_bytes(value: str) -> int:
201	        """Convert an rsync progress figure (e.g. '9.53G', '317K', '80,153,795') to bytes.
202	
203	        Two shapes occur in --info=progress2 output:
204	        - the plain byte counter, which rsync thousands-groups with a
205	          locale-dependent separator (',' on C/en_US, '.' on nl_BE-style),
206	          e.g. '80,153,795';
207	        - a human-readable figure with a K/M/G/T suffix (only when rsync runs
208	          with -h), e.g. '9.53G', where the '.' is a decimal point.
209	
210	        The parser strips both grouping separators so any locale's counter parses:
211	        the byte count is best-effort progress metadata (WR-01), not sync-critical.
212	
213	        Multipliers: K=1024, M=1024**2, G=1024**3, T=1024**4.
214	        """
215	        multipliers: dict[str, int] = {"K": 1024, "M": 1024**2, "G": 1024**3, "T": 1024**4}
216	        if value and value[-1].upper() in multipliers:
217	            # Suffixed figure carries a single decimal point; strip any comma grouping.
218	            return int(float(value[:-1].replace(",", "")) * multipliers[value[-1].upper()])
219	        # Plain integer counter: every '.'/',' is a thousands separator.
220	        return int(value.replace(".", "").replace(",", "") or "0")
221	
222	    def _active_folders(self) -> list[FolderEntry]:
223	        """Return only enabled folder entries from config."""
224	        return [
225	            FolderEntry(
226	                path=f["path"],
227	                enabled=f.get("enabled", True),
228	                filter_file=f.get("filter_file"),
229	            )
230	            for f in self.context.config["folders"]
231	            if f.get("enabled", True)
232	        ]
233	
234	    @classmethod
235	    @override
236	    def describe_first_sync_scope(cls, config: dict[str, Any]) -> FirstSyncScope | None:
237	        """Describe the enabled folder paths this job would overwrite on a first sync.
238	
239	        Reproduces the same enabled-folder filter as `_active_folders`, but operates
240	        on a raw config dict (this is a classmethod, called before job instances
241	        exist) rather than `self.context.config`: an entry is in scope when it is a
242	        dict, its `enabled` (default True) is truthy, and its `path` is a str.
243	        """
244	        folders = config.get("folders", [])
245	        paths = [
246	            f["path"]
247	            for f in folders
248	            if isinstance(f, dict) and f.get("enabled", True) and isinstance(f.get("path"), str)
249	        ]
250	        if not paths:
251	            return None
252	        return FirstSyncScope(
253	            job_name=cls.name,
254	            scope_items=paths,
255	            mechanism="rsync --delete",
256	        )
257	
258	    async def validate(self) -> list[ValidationError]:
259	        """Validate system state before executing the folder sync.
260	
261	        Checks (in order):
262	        1. `sudo rsync --version` succeeds on source and target.
263	        2. `acl` package is installed on source and target (required for -A flag;
264	           without it rsync silently drops ACLs — RESEARCH Pitfall 5).
265	        3. Each active folder path exists on the source; if a folder configures a
266	           `filter_file`, its (expanded) path must also exist on the source, else
267	           a configured-but-absent filter file could silently degrade to a full
268	           `--delete` mirror with no filter rules applied.
269	        4. Source and target share the same CPU architecture (`uname -m`): the
270	           pc-switcher install mirrors as arch-specific binaries (ADR-017), so a
271	           heterogeneous fleet is unsupported.
272	
273	        Returns the list of validation errors (empty on success).
274	        """
275	        errors: list[ValidationError] = []
276	
277	        # --- Step 0: source/target CPU architecture match ---
278	        # The pc-switcher install (uv tool venv + interpreter) mirrors to the target
279	        # as arch-specific binaries (ADR-017). A heterogeneous fleet would ship a
280	        # wrong-arch interpreter that dies at exec time with a cryptic
281	        # "cannot execute: required file not found"; refuse it up front. Fail open if
282	        # either `uname -m` cannot be read (never determined arch → nothing to compare).
283	        src_arch = await self.source.run_command("uname -m")
284	        tgt_arch = await self.target.run_command("uname -m", login_shell=False)
285	        if src_arch.success and tgt_arch.success and src_arch.stdout.strip() != tgt_arch.stdout.strip():
286	            errors.append(
287	                self._validation_error(
288	                    Host.TARGET,
289	                    f"source/target CPU architecture mismatch "
290	                    f"({src_arch.stdout.strip()} vs {tgt_arch.stdout.strip()}): pc-switcher mirrors "
291	                    f"arch-specific binaries and does not support a heterogeneous fleet",
292	                )
293	            )
294	
295	        # --- Step 1: sudo rsync availability ---
296	        result = await self.source.run_command("sudo rsync --version")
297	        if result.exit_code != 0:
298	            errors.append(
299	                self._validation_error(
300	                    Host.SOURCE,
301	                    "sudo rsync is not available on source (required for metadata-preserving transfer)",
302	                )
303	            )
304	
305	        result = await self.target.run_command("sudo rsync --version", login_shell=False)
306	        if result.exit_code != 0:
307	            errors.append(
308	                self._validation_error(
309	                    Host.TARGET,
310	                    "sudo rsync is not available on target (required for metadata-preserving transfer)",
311	                )
312	            )
313	
314	        # --- Step 2: acl package on both ends ---
315	        result = await self.source.run_command("dpkg -l acl | grep -q '^ii'")
316	        if result.exit_code != 0:
317	            errors.append(
318	                self._validation_error(
319	                    Host.SOURCE,
320	                    "acl package is not installed on source "
321	                    "(required for -A flag; without it rsync silently drops ACLs)",
322	                )
323	            )
324	
325	        result = await self.target.run_command("dpkg -l acl | grep -q '^ii'", login_shell=False)
326	        if result.exit_code != 0:
327	            errors.append(
328	                self._validation_error(
329	                    Host.TARGET,
330	                    "acl package is not installed on target "
331	                    "(required for -A flag; without it rsync silently drops ACLs)",
332	                )
333	            )
334	
335	        # --- Step 3: active folder existence on source, and filter_file existence ---
336	        for folder in self._active_folders():
337	            quoted = shlex.quote(folder.path)
338	            result = await self.source.run_command(f"test -d {quoted}")
339	            if result.exit_code != 0:
340	                errors.append(
341	                    self._validation_error(
342	                        Host.SOURCE,
343	                        f"folder {folder.path!r} does not exist on source",
344	                    )
345	                )
346	
347	            expanded_filter_file = folder.expanded_filter_file()
348	            if expanded_filter_file is not None:
349	                # Check the EXPANDED path so no literal `~` reaches the shell (mirrors
350	                # the folder existence check above for consistency and correct host).
351	                quoted_filter = shlex.quote(expanded_filter_file)
352	                result = await self.source.run_command(f"test -f {quoted_filter}")
353	                if result.exit_code != 0:
354	                    errors.append(
355	                        self._validation_error(
356	                            Host.SOURCE,
357	                            f"filter_file {folder.filter_file!r} for folder {folder.path!r} does not exist on source",
358	                        )
359	                    )
360	
361	        return errors
362	
363	    @staticmethod
364	    def _runtime_exclude_filters(folder_path: str) -> list[str]:
365	        """rsync `--filter` args protecting pc-switcher's own runtime state.
366	
367	        The runtime files (`_RUNTIME_EXCLUDE_RELPATHS`) live under the invoking user's
368	        home directory. When `folder_path` contains that home (e.g. syncing `/home`), each
369	        protected path is emitted as a root-anchored rsync pattern relative to the transfer
370	        root, so it matches exactly one location and can never be re-exposed by a user
371	        include rule. When home is outside `folder_path` (e.g. `/root` synced by a normal
372	        user) there is nothing to protect and the list is empty.
373	
374	        The editor state DBs are excluded separately (`_vscode_state_exclude_filters`);
375	        they are owned by `vscode_state_sync`, not folder_sync.
376	        """
377	        try:
378	            rel = Path.home().relative_to(folder_path.rstrip("/") or "/")
379	        except ValueError:
380	            return []
381	        prefix = "/" if str(rel) == "." else f"/{rel}/"
382	        return [f"--filter={shlex.quote(f'- {prefix}{sub}')}" for sub in _RUNTIME_EXCLUDE_RELPATHS]
383	
384	    @staticmethod
385	    def _vscode_state_exclude_filters(folder_path: str) -> list[str]:
386	        """rsync `--filter` args excluding the VS Code state DBs that fall under `folder_path`.
387	
388	        The absolute paths come from `vscode_state_exclude_paths()` — `vscode_state_sync`
389	        owns which DBs to exclude (the invoking user's, resolved at call time); folder_sync
390	        only translates each absolute path into a root-anchored, first-match exclude
391	        relative to the transfer root. A path outside `folder_path` (e.g. the invoking
392	        user's `/home/<user>/…` DB when syncing `/root`) is skipped. Emitted GLOBAL-FIRST,
393	        before the user filter surfaces, so no `+` rule can re-expose the DBs. Scope is the
394	        invoking user only (ADR-018); folder_sync holds no VS Code/home knowledge itself.
395	        """
396	        root = Path(folder_path.rstrip("/") or "/")
397	        filters: list[str] = []
398	        for abs_path in vscode_state_exclude_paths():
399	            try:
400	                rel = abs_path.relative_to(root)
401	            except ValueError:
402	                continue  # DB not under this folder — nothing to exclude here.
403	            filters.append(f"--filter={shlex.quote(f'- /{rel}')}")
404	        return filters
405	
406	    def _transport_args(self) -> list[str]:
407	        """Build the rsync `-e <ssh>` transport and `--rsync-path='sudo rsync'` args.
408	
409	        Shared by the main mirror (`_build_rsync_cmd`) and the filter pre-seed pass
410	        (`_build_preseed_cmd`) so both use identical SSH credentials and target-side
411	        sudo elevation.
412	
413	        SSH transport note: `sudo -E rsync` runs rsync as root, and the ssh it spawns
414	        also runs as root.  OpenSSH resolves `~/.ssh` from the running uid's passwd
415	        entry (root → /root/.ssh) regardless of the $HOME variable.  Passing explicit
416	        credentials (-l, -i, -o UserKnownHostsFile=, -F) with the invoking user's paths
417	        is the correct fix; relying on $HOME does NOT work (empirically verified on
418	        target VMs: ssh fails with "Host key verification failed" / rsync exit 255
419	        without explicit credentials).
420	        """
421	        ssh_dir = Path.home() / ".ssh"
422	        target_user = self.context.target_username or getpass.getuser()
423	
424	        ssh_tokens: list[str] = ["ssh", "-T", "-q", "-l", target_user]
425	
426	        # -F before host-name flags so it takes effect for all connection params.
427	        config_file = ssh_dir / "config"
428	        if config_file.exists():
429	            ssh_tokens += ["-F", str(config_file)]
430	
431	        known_hosts_file = ssh_dir / "known_hosts"
432	        if known_hosts_file.exists():
433	            ssh_tokens += ["-o", f"UserKnownHostsFile={known_hosts_file}"]
434	
435	        # Offer all default key types that exist; ssh tries each in order.
436	        # SSH_AUTH_SOCK (preserved by sudo -E) is also available when an agent
437	        # is running, but explicit -i keys cover the no-agent case.
438	        for key_name in ("id_ed25519", "id_ecdsa", "id_rsa"):
439	            key_file = ssh_dir / key_name
440	            if key_file.exists():
441	                ssh_tokens += ["-i", str(key_file)]
442	
443	        # shlex.join quotes individual tokens (handles spaces in paths).
444	        # shlex.quote wraps the assembled command for the outer -e argument.
445	        ssh_cmd = shlex.join(ssh_tokens)
446	        # Root on target via passwordless sudo scoped to /usr/bin/rsync (D-05).
447	        return [f"-e {shlex.quote(ssh_cmd)}", f"--rsync-path={shlex.quote('sudo rsync')}"]
448	
449	    async def _needs_seeding_pass(self, folder: FolderEntry) -> bool:
450	        """Whether a no-delete seeding pass must run before the deleting mirror.
451	
452	        A dir-merge rule is read from the *receiver* during the delete scan, so the
453	        deleting mirror protects the target correctly only when every source
454	        `.pcswitcher-filter` is already present and byte-identical on the target.  In
455	        the steady state that already holds — filter files sync like any other file —
456	        so a single pass is safe and the whole tree is walked only once.  A seeding
457	        pass is needed only when a per-directory filter was added or changed on the
458	        source (or on a first sync): then the source's filter files are not yet
459	        reflected on the target and the mirror must align them first.
460	
461	        Detects this by hashing just the (few, small) `.pcswitcher-filter` files on
462	        each side and comparing — far cheaper than the second full rsync pass it
463	        avoids.  Short-circuits when the source has none (the common case — the
464	        default config uses only the central `filter_file`), skipping the target
465	        round-trip.  Runs as root, like the mirror, so filter files under directories
466	        the invoking user cannot read are still seen and hashed.  Target-only extra
467	        filter files do not force a seeding pass (they only ever add protection).
468	        """
469	        path = shlex.quote(folder.path.rstrip("/") or "/")
470	        manifest_cmd = f"sudo find {path} -name .pcswitcher-filter -exec sha256sum {{}} +"
471	        src = await self.source.run_command(manifest_cmd)
472	        src_manifest = set(src.stdout.splitlines())
473	        if not src_manifest:
474	            return False  # no per-directory filters on the source -> nothing to seed
475	        tgt = await self.target.run_command(manifest_cmd, login_shell=False)
476	        tgt_manifest = set(tgt.stdout.splitlines())
477	        # Seed unless every source filter file is present and identical on the target.
478	        return not src_manifest.issubset(tgt_manifest)
479	
480	    def _build_rsync_cmd(self, folder: FolderEntry, dry_run: bool, delete: bool = True) -> str:
481	        """Build the rsync shell command for syncing a single folder.
482	
483	        Produces a command that:
484	        - Runs as root on source via `sudo -E rsync` (preserves SSH_AUTH_SOCK
485	          so the ssh agent socket remains accessible).
486	        - Elevates to root on target via `--rsync-path='sudo rsync'` over the
487	          normal-user SSH connection (D-05); root SSH login stays disabled.
488	        - Uses the D-13 flag baseline: -aAXHS + --numeric-ids + --delete.
489	        - Adds --dry-run only when `dry_run` is True (D-12).
490	        - Omits --delete when `delete` is False: `execute` runs a no-delete seeding
491	          pass first (when the tree has per-directory filters) so every source
492	          `.pcswitcher-filter` reaches the target before the deleting mirror — a
493	          dir-merge rule only protects the target once the filter file is on the
494	          receiver, else --delete would remove (not protect) the files it names.
495	        - Never includes --delete-excluded (excluded files must survive on target
496	          — D-06) or --checksum (rsync's built-in verification is trusted — D-14).
497	
498	        All config-derived values (folder path, exclude patterns, target hostname)
499	        are shlex.quote'd to prevent shell injection (T-05-01).
500	
501	        The SSH transport and target-side sudo elevation are built by
502	        `_transport_args`.  The filter chain (runtime excludes → central merge →
503	        dir-merge) is identical with and without --delete, so both passes respect the
504	        central `filter_file` and per-directory `.pcswitcher-filter` files exactly.
505	        """
506	        parts = [
507	            "sudo",
508	            "-E",
509	            "rsync",
510	            "-aAXHS",
511	            "--numeric-ids",
512	            # Build the whole file list before transferring anything, so
513	            # --info=progress2 reports against the real total from its first line.
514	            # Under rsync's default incremental recursion the walk and the transfer
515	            # interleave: the percentage is bytes-done over bytes-known-*so far* and
516	            # walks backwards as the walk discovers more tree, and `to-chk` only
517	            # appears for the handful of files still queued when the walk ends
518	            # (measured on a 14.6 GB / 154k-file tree: 138,535 ir-chk lines carrying
519	            # 99.9% of the bytes, then 1,159 to-chk lines in the final second).
520	            # Cost is bounded by the walk: measured on a 1.59M-entry /home, +0.5s wall
521	            # and +80 MB RSS, with no progress output at all until the walk finishes.
522	            "--no-inc-recursive",
523	            "--info=progress2",
524	            f"--out-format={shlex.quote('%i %n%L')}",
525	            "--partial",
526	            "--mkpath",
527	        ]
528	
529	        if delete:
530	            parts.append("--delete")
531	
532	        if dry_run:
533	            parts.append("--dry-run")
534	
535	        # SSH transport + target-side sudo elevation (shared with the pre-seed pass).
536	        parts.extend(self._transport_args())
537	
538	        # GLOBAL-FIRST filter precedence: the un-overridable excludes come first —
539	        # pc-switcher's runtime state (ADR-017) followed by the VS Code state DBs
540	        # (ADR-018); the folder's central `merge` filter (when configured) comes
541	        # next so its rules win over any per-directory file under first-match-wins;
542	        # the tree-wide `dir-merge /.pcswitcher-filter` is always last and gives
543	        # users a per-directory (gitignore-like) authoring surface. DO NOT add
544	        # --delete-excluded — excluded files (e.g. .ssh/id_*, .config/tailscale)
545	        # must survive on the target (D-06).
546	        parts.extend(self._runtime_exclude_filters(folder.path))
547	        parts.extend(self._vscode_state_exclude_filters(folder.path))
548	
549	        expanded_filter_file = folder.expanded_filter_file()
550	        if expanded_filter_file is not None:
551	            parts.append(f"--filter={shlex.quote(f'merge {expanded_filter_file}')}")
552	        parts.append(f"--filter={shlex.quote('dir-merge /.pcswitcher-filter')}")
553	
554	        # Source: trailing slash syncs contents, not the directory itself.
555	        src = shlex.quote(folder.path.rstrip("/") + "/")
556	        parts.append(src)
557	
558	        # Destination: <target_hostname>:<path>/ — rsync opens its own SSH
559	        # connection using ~/.ssh/config (ADR-002); the colon is rsync's own
560	        # remote-host separator, not a shell metacharacter.
561	        dst_raw = f"{self.context.target_hostname}:{folder.path.rstrip('/') + '/'}"
562	        parts.append(shlex.quote(dst_raw))
563	
564	        return " ".join(parts)
565	
566	    async def _stream_rsync(
567	        self,
568	        chunks: AsyncIterator[bytes],
569	        folder: FolderEntry,
570	        label: str,
571	    ) -> tuple[int, int, int]:
572	        """Stream rsync stdout, updating TUI progress and logging per-file events.
573	
574	        Handles `--info=progress2` output (carriage-return-delimited) and
575	        `--out-format` per-file lines (newline-delimited) in the same byte
576	        stream (RESEARCH Pitfall 2 — do not use readline-based iteration here).
577	
578	        Decoupled from the subprocess: consumes any `AsyncIterator[bytes]` so
579	        it can be unit-tested with a fake async generator.
580	
581	        Progress is reported per folder: each folder owns one bar for the whole run
582	        (tracked by path), and a pass restarts it at 0% and renames it with `label`
583	        rather than adding a bar of its own.  rsync is silent while it builds the file
584	        list (`--no-inc-recursive`, see `_build_rsync_cmd`), so the pass opens with a
585	        heartbeat and the bar only becomes determinate once transfer starts.
586	
587	        Args:
588	            chunks: Async byte-chunk source (e.g. `proc.read_stdout_chunks()`).
589	            folder: The folder being synced (used for log context).
590	            label: Pass name shown next to the folder in the TUI (`prep` / `full`).
591	
592	        Returns:
593	            Tuple of (files_transferred, bytes_transferred, files_deleted).
594	            bytes_transferred is a best-effort count based on the last progress
595	            line (progress2 doesn't expose a precise cumulative byte count).
596	        """
597	        files_xfr = 0
598	        bytes_xfr = 0
599	        files_deleted = 0
600	        files_scanned = 0
601	        buf = b""
602	
603	        where = _pass_display(folder.path, label)
604	        self._report_progress(ProgressUpdate(heartbeat=True, item=f"{where} — building file list", track=folder.path))
605	
606	        async for chunk in chunks:
607	            buf += chunk
608	            # Split on both \r and \n to handle progress2 (uses \r) and
609	            # per-file --out-format lines (use \n) in the same stream.
610	            parts = re.split(rb"[\r\n]", buf)
611	            buf = parts[-1]  # Keep incomplete trailing fragment for next chunk.
612	            for fragment in parts[:-1]:
613	                line = fragment.decode(errors="replace").strip()
614	                if not line:
615	                    continue
616	                m = _PROGRESS2_RE.search(line)
617	                if m:
618	                    # Progress2 line: update TUI (D-15) and capture transferred bytes (WR-01).
619	                    # Groups: 1=size 2=percent (unused, see below) 3=files_xfr
620	                    #         4=chk prefix 5=to-check 6=total
621	                    # Last progress line wins — rsync emits these as running totals so the
622	                    # final line is the best approximation of the cumulative byte count.
623	                    bytes_xfr = self._parse_size_to_bytes(m.group(1))
624	                    files_xfr = int(m.group(3))
625	                    scanning = m.group(4) == "ir"
626	                    remaining = int(m.group(5))
627	                    listed = int(m.group(6))
628	                    if scanning:
629	                        # Fallback path only: --no-inc-recursive means rsync should never
630	                        # emit ir-chk.  If it does anyway, `listed` is the entry count
631	                        # discovered *so far* rather than a total, so neither it nor the
632	                        # percentage may be shown — report the scanned count alone and let
633	                        # the TUI render an indeterminate bar.  The count is clamped
634	                        # monotonic because `listed` grows under the walk.
635	                        files_scanned = max(files_scanned, listed - remaining)
636	                        self._report_progress(
637	                            ProgressUpdate(
638	                                current=files_scanned,
639	                                item=f"{where} — scanning {files_scanned:,} files ({format_bytes(bytes_xfr)})",
640	                                track=folder.path,
641	                            )
642	                        )
643	                    else:
644	                        # Full file list known, so `listed` is the real total and the
645	                        # checked-file count drives the bar.  rsync's own percentage
646	                        # (group 2) is deliberately ignored: it is bytes-sent over the
647	                        # total size of the whole tree, so an incremental sync — the
648	                        # common case — reads 0% from start to finish (measured: 200 of
649	                        # 154,022 files re-sent, 14.4 MB of 14.6 GB, 0% on every line).
650	                        # Counting files instead spans 0-100% in both cases; the cost is
651	                        # that one huge file advances the bar as little as one tiny one,
652	                        # so the byte figure stays in the label.
653	                        checked = listed - remaining
654	                        self._report_progress(
655	                            ProgressUpdate(
656	                                percent=100 if listed == 0 else min(100, checked * 100 // listed),
657	                                item=f"{where} — {checked:,}/{listed:,} files, {format_bytes(bytes_xfr)}",
658	                                track=folder.path,
659	                            )
660	                        )
661	                elif line[0] in (">", "<", "*", ".", "c", "h"):
662	                    # Per-file --out-format line (format: "%i %n%L") — log at FULL (D-16).
663	                    # Change-type characters (rsync %i first char):
664	                    #   > sent to remote   < received from remote   * special message
665	                    #   . attribute-only   c created (dir/symlink/device)   h hard link (IN-03)
666	                    # Full itemize-code reference: docs/reading-sync-logs.md
667	                    self._log(Host.SOURCE, LogLevel.FULL, f"{folder.path}: {line}")
668	                    if line.startswith("*deleting"):
669	                        files_deleted += 1
670	
671	        # Flush any remaining fragment that didn't end with \r or \n.
672	        if buf:
673	            line = buf.decode(errors="replace").strip()
674	            if line and line[0] in (">", "<", "*", ".", "c", "h"):
675	                self._log(Host.SOURCE, LogLevel.FULL, f"{folder.path}: {line}")
676	                if line.startswith("*deleting"):
677	                    files_deleted += 1
678	
679	        return files_xfr, bytes_xfr, files_deleted
680	
681	    async def _run_rsync_pass(self, cmd: str, folder: FolderEntry, label: str) -> tuple[int, int, int]:
682	        """Run one rsync pass: spawn, stream progress/logs, and raise on non-zero exit.
683	
684	        Spawns rsync as an async subprocess (ADR-005 — no blocking calls), streams
685	        stdout through `_stream_rsync` for TUI progress (D-15) and per-file FULL logs
686	        (D-16), then checks the exit code.  Returns the pass's
687	        (files_transferred, bytes_transferred, files_deleted).  Shared by the no-delete
688	        seeding pass and the deleting mirror in `execute`, which name themselves via
689	        `label` (`prep` / `full`).
690	        """
691	        proc = await self.source.start_process(cmd)
692	        files_transferred, bytes_transferred, files_deleted = await self._stream_rsync(
693	            proc.read_stdout_chunks(), folder, label
694	        )
695	        result = await proc.wait_result()
696	        if result.exit_code != 0:
697	            self._log(
698	                Host.SOURCE,
699	                LogLevel.CRITICAL,
700	                f"rsync failed for {folder.path!r}: {result.stderr.strip()}",
701	                exit_code=result.exit_code,
702	            )
703	            raise RuntimeError(f"rsync failed for {folder.path!r} with exit code {result.exit_code}")
704	        # A pass with nothing to transfer (steady state, or any dry-run) races through
705	        # its progress output, so close the bar explicitly rather than leaving it wherever
706	        # the last line landed — or pulsing, if no line ever arrived.
707	        where = _pass_display(folder.path, label)
708	        self._report_progress(ProgressUpdate(percent=100, item=where, track=folder.path))
709	        return files_transferred, bytes_transferred, files_deleted
710	
711	    async def execute(self) -> None:
712	        """Sync each active folder via rsync-over-SSH.
713	
714	        For each active folder (D-10):
715	        1. When this is a real sync and the source's per-directory `.pcswitcher-filter`
716	           files are not already reflected on the target (`_needs_seeding_pass`), runs
717	           a seeding pass first — the same mirror WITHOUT `--delete` — so every source
718	           `.pcswitcher-filter` reaches the target before the deleting mirror.  A
719	           dir-merge rule only protects the target once the filter file is on the
720	           receiver, so without this the deleting mirror would remove (not protect)
721	           the files a per-dir rule names.  Both passes apply the identical filter
722	           chain, so the central `filter_file` and per-directory rules are respected
723	           exactly.  Skipped in dry-run (must not write to the target, D-12), which
724	           makes the dry-run deletion preview pessimistic for per-dir-protected paths
725	           (the safe direction), and skipped when the per-directory filters already
726	           match on both ends (the steady state — no seeding is needed).
727	        2. Runs the deleting mirror (D-13 baseline, D-11 filters, D-12 dry-run toggle).
728	        3. On non-zero exit from either pass, logs CRITICAL and raises RuntimeError.
729	        """
730	        folders = self._active_folders()
731	
732	        for folder in folders:
733	            prefix = "[dry-run] " if self.context.dry_run else ""
734	            self._log(
735	                Host.SOURCE,
736	                LogLevel.INFO,
737	                f"{prefix}Syncing {folder.path!r} → {self.context.target_hostname!r}",
738	                session_id=self.context.session_id,
739	            )
740	
741	            seed_files = 0
742	            seed_bytes = 0
743	            if not self.context.dry_run and await self._needs_seeding_pass(folder):
744	                self._log(
745	                    Host.SOURCE,
746	                    LogLevel.FULL,
747	                    f"Seeding per-directory filter files for {folder.path!r} (no-delete pass)",
748	                )
749	                seed_files, seed_bytes, _ = await self._run_rsync_pass(
750	                    self._build_rsync_cmd(folder, dry_run=False, delete=False), folder, PASS_PREP
751	                )
752	
753	            # Deleting mirror (or, in dry-run, the read-only preview).
754	            mirror_files, mirror_bytes, files_deleted = await self._run_rsync_pass(
755	                self._build_rsync_cmd(folder, self.context.dry_run, delete=True), folder, PASS_FULL
756	            )
757	
758	            # On a seeding run the bulk transfer happened in the first pass and the
759	            # mirror transfers ~nothing; sum so the summary reflects the real work.
760	            files_transferred = seed_files + mirror_files
761	            bytes_transferred = seed_bytes + mirror_bytes
762	
763	            # Per-folder summary (D-16). Human-readable size at INFO; exact byte
764	            # count kept at DEBUG for precise diagnostics (#189).
765	            self._log(
766	                Host.SOURCE,
767	                LogLevel.INFO,
768	                f"{prefix}Completed sync of {folder.path!r}: "
769	                f"{files_transferred} files transferred, "
770	                f"{format_bytes(bytes_transferred)}, "
771	                f"{files_deleted} deletions",
772	            )
773	            self._log(
774	                Host.SOURCE,
775	                LogLevel.DEBUG,
776	                f"{prefix}Transferred {bytes_transferred} bytes for {folder.path!r}",
777	            )
778	
779	        # No job-level completion update: every pass already closes its own bar at 100%
780	        # (`_run_rsync_pass`), and an unlabelled one here would only strip the folder
781	        # and pass name off the finished bar.

content.text:
Confirmed from the code — your reading of the label is right, but the pass does not do what its name suggests.

`src/pcswitcher/jobs/folder_sync.py:743-751`: the seeding pass is built with `self._build_rsync_cmd(folder, dry_run=False, delete=False)` — the **exact same mirror command as the real pass, only without `--delete`**. Same `-aAXHS`, same filter chain, same source/dest. It is not restricted to `.pcswitcher-filter` files.

So yes: **all files were transferred in the first pass.** The second pass then re-walked the tree, found everything already in place, and only had deletions to do — hence the blitz.

That is the intent as coded, and the comments say so explicitly:

- `folder_sync.py:758-759`: "On a seeding run the bulk transfer happened in the first pass and the mirror transfers ~nothing; sum so the summary reflects the real work."
- The *purpose* of the pass (`_needs_seeding_pass`, `folder_sync.py:449-478`) is narrow: get every source `.pcswitcher-filter` onto the target **before** the deleting mirror runs, because a `dir-merge` rule is read from the *receiver* during the delete scan. Without it, `--delete` would delete the files a per-dir rule is meant to protect.

So the naming ("prep") reflects the *reason* the pass exists, not its *scope*. The scope is a full no-delete sync.

Two things worth noting:

1. The pass only fires when source and target `.pcswitcher-filter` hashes differ (or on first sync). Steady state → single pass. You presumably hit it because a per-dir filter changed, or this was a first sync to fleksi.
2. It could be much cheaper. To achieve its stated goal it only needs the filter files: `--include=*/ --include=.pcswitcher-filter --exclude=*` (no `--delete`), which walks the tree but transfers kilobytes instead of gigabytes. The current form works, but it front-loads the entire transfer into a pass labelled `prep` — which is exactly the confusion you just ran into, and it also means the double tree-walk cost is paid on the full data set.

Want me to open an issue for narrowing the seeding pass to filter files only?

Currently, we show "(prep)" for the first pass and nothing for the second pass. This naming is wrong. The first pass is the full no-delete pass, the second pass is the delete-pass.
Than naming should be better in code and in tui. What do you propose? Wait for my approval

Read the code; one wrinkle drives the proposal.

The second pass is only "the delete pass" **when the first pass ran**. In the steady state (`_needs_seeding_pass` false, the common case) there is a single pass that both transfers and deletes. So a fixed two-name scheme mislabels the single-pass run.

Proposal: three labels, not two.

| Case | Pass | Constant | TUI |
|---|---|---|---|
| Single pass (steady state) | transfer + delete | `PASS_MIRROR` | `/home` |
| Two passes | 1st: full copy, no delete | `PASS_COPY` | `/home (copy)` |
| | 2nd: delete-only in practice | `PASS_DELETE` | `/home (delete)` |

Code changes:

- `folder_sync.py:63-64` — replace `PASS_PREP = "prep"` / `PASS_FULL = "full"` with `PASS_COPY = "copy"`, `PASS_DELETE = "delete"`, `PASS_MIRROR = "mirror"`.
- `_pass_display` (`:67-74`) — bare path for `PASS_MIRROR`, `f"{path} ({label})"` otherwise. Rewrite the docstring: the rule is no longer "the pass that always runs is bare" but "a lone pass does the whole job, so it needs no qualifier; when the work is split, each half is named".
- `execute` (`:743-756`) — hoist the `_needs_seeding_pass` result into a local, pass `PASS_COPY` to the seeding call, and choose `PASS_DELETE if seeded else PASS_MIRROR` for the mirror call.
- Docstrings in `execute`, `_run_rsync_pass`, `_stream_rsync`, `_needs_seeding_pass` currently say "seeding pass" / "prep" — retitle to "copy pass". Keep the *why* (dir-merge is read from the receiver) intact; only the name changes.
- Tests referencing `PASS_PREP`/`PASS_FULL` or the `(prep)` string get updated.

Wording alternatives if you dislike `copy`/`delete`: `add`/`remove`, or `transfer`/`prune`. I'd take `copy`/`delete` — `delete` matches the rsync flag the user can grep for, and `copy` states plainly that the pass moves data (the thing that surprised you).

Not proposed here, but flagged: this only renames. The `copy` pass still transfers the whole tree when it only needs `.pcswitcher-filter` files. Separate issue.

Waiting for your approval.

input.old_string:
# The two rsync passes a folder can run (see `execute`): the no-delete pass that ships
# per-directory filter files, and the deleting mirror.  Both share that folder's
# progress bar (tracked by path) and only rename it, so the run shows one bar per
# configured folder — finished folders stay at 100%.
PASS_PREP = "prep"
PASS_FULL = "full"


def _pass_display(path: str, label: str) -> str:
    """Name a pass for the TUI.

    The deleting mirror is the pass that always runs, so it shows the bare folder path;
    only the optional seeding pass is qualified, so its bar cannot be mistaken for the
    real sync of that folder.
    """
    return path if label == PASS_FULL else f"{path} ({label})"
input.new_string:
# How a folder's rsync work is split (see `execute`).  In the steady state one pass
# both transfers and deletes (PASS_MIRROR).  When per-directory filter files must reach
# the target first, the work splits in two: a full no-delete copy (PASS_COPY) followed by
# the deleting mirror (PASS_DELETE), which by then has only deletions left to do.
# All passes of a folder share that folder's progress bar (tracked by path) and only
# rename it, so the run shows one bar per configured folder — finished folders stay
# at 100%.
PASS_COPY = "copy"
PASS_DELETE = "delete"
PASS_MIRROR = "mirror"


def _pass_display(path: str, label: str) -> str:
    """Name a pass for the TUI.

    A lone pass does the folder's whole job, so it needs no qualifier and shows the bare
    path.  When the work is split across two passes each half is named, so neither bar
    can be mistaken for the complete sync of that folder.
    """
    return path if label == PASS_MIRROR else f"{path} ({label})"

class TestNeedsSeedingPass:

        target_rc = AsyncMock(return_value=CommandResult(exit_code=0, stdout=target_out, stderr=""))
        ctx.target.run_command = target_rc
        return FolderSyncJob(ctx), target_rc

    `.pcswitcher-filter` onto the target while respecting the full filter chain; the deleting pass
    then protects correctly. These tests reproduce both passes with real rsync (no --dry-run) and
    pin the single-pass bug plus the two-pass fix for the delete AND update cases.
    """

    @staticmethod
    def _no_delete_pass(src: Path, dst: Path) -> None:
        # Mirrors _build_rsync_cmd(delete=False): full filter chain, no --delete (seeds filter files).
        subprocess.run(
            ["rsync", "-a", "--filter=dir-merge /.pcswitcher-filter", f"{src}/", f"{dst}/"],
            check=True,
            capture_output=True,
            text=True,
        )

    @staticmethod
    def _mirror(src: Path, dst: Path) -> None:
        # Mirrors _build_rsync_cmd(delete=True): the real --delete pass with dir-merge.
        subprocess.run(
            ["rsync", "-a", "--delete", "--filter=dir-merge /.pcswitcher-filter", f"{src}/", f"{dst}/"],
            check=True,
            capture_output=True,
            text=True,
        )

    def test_single_pass_deletes_the_excluded_target_file(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:
        """Without the seeding pass, the deleting mirror DELETES the rule-excluded target file (the bug)."""
        src = tmp_path / "src"
        dst = tmp_path / "dst"
        _write(src / "proj/.pcswitcher-filter", "- secret.env\n")
        _write(dst / "proj/secret.env", "tgt-only-secret")  # target-only, rule-excluded
        self._mirror(src, dst)
        assert not (dst / "proj/secret.env").exists()

    def test_single_pass_protects_when_filter_already_on_target(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:
        """Steady state: with the .pcswitcher-filter already on the target, ONE deleting pass protects it.

        This is exactly what _needs_seeding_pass relies on to skip the seeding pass when the source
        and target filter files already match — the common case after a first successful sync.
        """
        src = tmp_path / "src"
        dst = tmp_path / "dst"
        _write(src / "proj/.pcswitcher-filter", "- secret.env\n")
        _write(dst / "proj/.pcswitcher-filter", "- secret.env\n")  # already present on the target
        _write(dst / "proj/secret.env", "tgt-only-secret")
        self._mirror(src, dst)  # single --delete pass, no seeding
        assert (dst / "proj/secret.env").read_text() == "tgt-only-secret"

    def test_two_pass_protects_a_target_only_excluded_file(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:
        """delete case: seeding pass then mirror keeps a target-only rule-excluded file."""
        src = tmp_path / "src"
        dst = tmp_path / "dst"
        _write(src / "proj/.pcswitcher-filter", "- secret.env\n")
        _write(src / "proj/keep.txt", "keep-src")
        _write(dst / "proj/secret.env", "tgt-only-secret")  # target-only
        self._no_delete_pass(src, dst)
        self._mirror(src, dst)
        assert (dst / "proj/secret.env").read_text() == "tgt-only-secret"  # protected
        assert (dst / "proj/keep.txt").read_text() == "keep-src"  # non-excluded still syncs
        assert (dst / "proj/.pcswitcher-filter").exists()  # filter file itself transferred

    def test_two_pass_does_not_overwrite_or_delete_an_excluded_update(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:
        """update case: source has a rule-excluded file with NEW content, target has OLD; target keeps OLD.

        This is the case that fails in a single pass (the target file is deleted). The seeding pass
        puts the filter onto the target first, so the deleting mirror neither overwrites nor deletes it.
        """
        src = tmp_path / "src"
        dst = tmp_path / "dst"
        _write(src / "proj/.pcswitcher-filter", "- secret.env\n")
        _write(src / "proj/secret.env", "SRC-NEW")  # excluded on the source (not sent)

## Problem

The TUI labelled the first rsync pass `(prep)` and left the second bare, which reads as "the first pass prepares, the second syncs". It is the other way round: the first pass is built by `_build_rsync_cmd(delete=False)` — the identical full mirror minus `--delete` — so it transfers the entire tree, and the deleting mirror that follows has only deletions left to do. Observed live: multiple GB moved under `(prep)`, then the unlabelled pass finished instantly.

## Change

Naming only, no behaviour change.

The split into two passes is conditional (it happens only when the source's per-directory `.pcswitcher-filter` files are not yet on the target), so a two-name scheme would mislabel the common single-pass run. Three labels instead:

| Case | Pass | Constant | TUI |
|---|---|---|---|
| Steady state | one pass, transfers + deletes | `PASS_MIRROR` | `/home` |
| Split run | 1st: full copy, no delete | `PASS_COPY` | `/home (copy)` |
| | 2nd: deletions in practice | `PASS_DELETE` | `/home (delete)` |

A lone pass does the folder's whole job so it stays unqualified; when the work is split, both halves are named so neither bar can be mistaken for the complete sync.

Also: `_needs_seeding_pass` → `_needs_copy_pass`, "seeding pass" → "copy pass" throughout docstrings and tests, and `execute`'s docstring now states outright that the copy pass is not limited to filter files.

## Not in scope

The copy pass still transfers the whole tree when its stated purpose only needs the `.pcswitcher-filter` files. Narrowing it is a separate change.

## Verification

`ruff check` / `ruff format` / `basedpyright` clean; `pytest` 705 passed; `tests/local_rsync` 8 passed. Integration tests not run locally — CI on this PR.

Draft PR: https://github.com/flaksit/pc-switcher/pull/201

Branch `refactor/198-folder-sync-pass-naming` (branched off `origin/main` — the earlier `fix/198-…` branch was already merged via #199).

What landed:

- `PASS_PREP`/`PASS_FULL` → `PASS_COPY`/`PASS_DELETE`/`PASS_MIRROR`; `_pass_display` now leaves only `PASS_MIRROR` unqualified.
- `execute` hoists the split decision into a local and picks `PASS_DELETE if split else PASS_MIRROR`; locals `seed_files`/`seed_bytes` → `copy_files`/`copy_bytes`.
- `_needs_seeding_pass` → `_needs_copy_pass`; "seeding" retired across docstrings, tests and ADR-018's one reference.
- `execute`'s docstring now says plainly that the copy pass is the full transfer, not just filter files.

Checks: ruff + basedpyright clean, 705 unit tests and 8 local_rsync tests pass. Integration tests will run in CI on this PR (base is `main`, so they trigger).

Two things to note:

1. I left the commit without the `Co-Authored-By`/`Claude-Session` trailers — I missed them when writing it, and adding them now means amending, which your rules forbid unless you ask. Say the word if you want it amended.
2. The scope issue stands unfixed: the copy pass moves the whole tree when it only needs the `.pcswitcher-filter` files. I called it out as out-of-scope in the PR body. Want an issue opened for narrowing it?
